title: FlatList Optimization Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of React Native FlatList performance patterns, windowed rendering, and image caching.

questions.question: Why is FlatList more performant than ScrollView + .map() for long lists?

options.text: FlatList renders all items at startup to pre-cache them
FlatList uses windowed rendering — only items visible on screen (plus a buffer) are mounted at any time, recycling instances as the user scrolls
FlatList automatically compresses images to reduce memory usage
ScrollView does not support touch events, so FlatList must be used

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of providing a `getItemLayout` prop to FlatList?

options.text: It enables horizontal scrolling instead of vertical
It pre-computes all item positions so FlatList doesn't need to dynamically measure heights, enabling fast scrollToIndex and improving overall scroll performance
It automatically memoizes each item to prevent re-renders
It limits the list to a maximum number of rendered items

questions.question: Why should the renderItem function be wrapped in useCallback before passing to FlatList?

options.text: FlatList requires all props to be callback functions
A new function reference on every render causes all visible items to re-render; useCallback keeps the reference stable
useCallback makes the rendering happen on the native thread
Without useCallback, FlatList cannot call the renderItem function
Only load 50% of the total items in the dataset
Trigger the onEndReached callback when the user has scrolled past 50% of the entire list
Trigger the onEndReached callback when the user is within 50% of a screen-height from the bottom of the rendered content
Cache only 50% of items in the windowed buffer

questions.question: What advantage does react-native-fast-image have over the built-in Image component in lists?

options.text: It supports animated GIFs natively
It caches images to disk and uses better memory management, so images in list items are loaded from cache on scroll rather than re-fetched from the network
It renders images on the JS thread, avoiding bridge overhead
It requires no internet connection as it stores all images in the bundle
